title: Закон за защита на търговската тайна
description: Bulgaria's trade secrets protection legislation transposing Directive (EU) 2016/943 of the European Parliament and of the Council on the protection of undisclosed know-how and business information (trade secrets) against their unlawful acquisition, use, and disclosure.

provisions.title: Subject and scope
provisions.content: This Act regulates the conditions and procedure for the protection of trade secrets against their unlawful acquisition, use, and disclosure. This Act transposes the requirements of Directive (EU) 2016/943 of the European Parliament and of the Council of 8 June 2016 on the protection of undisclosed know-how and business information (trade secrets) against their unlawful acquisition, use and disclosure.

provisions.title: Trade secret defined
provisions.content: A trade secret means information which: is secret in the sense that it is not generally known among or readily accessible to persons within the circles that normally deal with the kind of information in question; has commercial value because it is secret; has been subject to reasonable steps under the circumstances by the person lawfully in control of the information to keep it secret. This definition aligns with Article 2(1) of Directive (EU) 2016/943.

provisions.title: Trade secret holder
provisions.content: A trade secret holder means any natural or legal person lawfully controlling a trade secret. The holder must demonstrate that reasonable steps have been taken to keep the information secret, including: restricting access to the information on a need-to-know basis; using non-disclosure agreements; implementing technical and organisational security measures; marking information as confidential.

provisions.title: Lawful acquisition
provisions.content: The acquisition of a trade secret shall be considered lawful when the trade secret is obtained by: independent discovery or creation; observation, study, disassembly or testing of a product or object that has been made available to the public or that is lawfully in the possession of the acquirer; exercise of the right of workers or workers' representatives to information and consultation in accordance with Union law and national law; any other practice which, under the circumstances, is in conformity with honest commercial practices.

provisions.title: Unlawful acquisition
provisions.content: The acquisition of a trade secret without the consent of the trade secret holder shall be considered unlawful whenever carried out by: unauthorised access to, appropriation of, or copying of any documents, objects, materials, substances or electronic files lawfully under the control of the trade secret holder and containing the trade secret or from which the trade secret can be deduced; any other conduct which, under the circumstances, is considered contrary to honest commercial practices.

provisions.title: Unlawful use and disclosure
provisions.content: The use or disclosure of a trade secret shall be considered unlawful whenever carried out, without the consent of the trade secret holder, by a person who has acquired the trade secret unlawfully; is in breach of a confidentiality agreement or any other duty not to disclose the trade secret; is in breach of a contractual or any other duty to limit the use of the trade secret.

provisions.title: Whistleblower protection
provisions.content: The acquisition, use or disclosure of a trade secret shall be considered lawful to the extent that it is required or allowed by Union law or national law, including: for the purpose of protecting a legitimate interest recognised by law; for revealing misconduct, wrongdoing or illegal activity, provided that the respondent acted for the purpose of protecting the general public interest (whistleblower exception); disclosure by workers to their representatives in the exercise of their functions.

provisions.title: Freedom of expression
provisions.content: The application of this Act shall not restrict the freedom of expression and information, including the freedom and pluralism of the media, as enshrined in Article 11 of the Charter of Fundamental Rights of the European Union. Measures, procedures and remedies under this Act shall respect the right to freedom of expression and shall not be used to restrict the lawful exercise of this right, particularly in investigative journalism.

provisions.title: Right to bring proceedings
provisions.content: A trade secret holder shall be entitled to bring proceedings before the competent court to seek measures, procedures and remedies provided for in this Act against any natural or legal person who has unlawfully acquired, used or disclosed a trade secret. Proceedings shall be brought within three years from the date on which the applicant became aware, or had reason to become aware, of the last event giving rise to the action.

provisions.title: Provisional and precautionary measures
provisions.content: The court may, at the request of the trade secret holder, issue provisional and precautionary measures, including: cessation of or prohibition against the use or disclosure of the trade secret on a provisional basis; prohibition of the production, offering, placing on the market, or use of infringing goods, or the importation, export, or storage of infringing goods; seizure or delivery up of the suspected infringing goods, including imported goods.

provisions.title: Preservation of confidentiality during proceedings
provisions.content: The court shall, at the request of a party or on its own initiative, take specific measures necessary to preserve the confidentiality of any trade secret or alleged trade secret used or referred to in the course of legal proceedings. Such measures may include: restricting access to documents containing trade secrets; restricting access to hearings; making available a non-confidential version of any judicial decision.

provisions.title: Injunctions and corrective measures
provisions.content: Where a judicial decision finds that the acquisition, use or disclosure of a trade secret was unlawful, the competent court may: order the cessation of or prohibition against the use or disclosure of the trade secret; order the prohibition of the production, offering, placing on the market, or use of infringing goods; order the destruction in whole or in part of any document, object, material, substance, or electronic file containing or embodying the trade secret.

provisions.title: Damages
provisions.content: The trade secret holder shall be entitled to compensation for damages suffered as a result of the unlawful acquisition, use or disclosure of the trade secret. The court shall take into account all appropriate factors, including: the negative economic consequences, including lost profits, which the injured party has suffered; any unfair profits made by the infringer; in appropriate cases, elements other than economic factors, such as the moral prejudice caused to the trade secret holder.

provisions.title: Relationship with other laws
provisions.content: The provisions of this Act shall not affect the protection of intellectual property rights under the Copyright and Related Rights Act, the Patents and Registration of Utility Models Act, and the Marks and Geographical Indications Act. This Act shall be without prejudice to the application of competition law rules, including the Protection of Competition Act.

definitions.definition: Information which is secret, has commercial value because it is secret, and has been subject to reasonable steps to keep it secret.
Any natural or legal person lawfully controlling a trade secret.
Goods the design, characteristics, functioning, production process or marketing of which significantly benefits from trade secrets unlawfully acquired, used or disclosed.
